framework.name: User Impersonation Is Current Default, Not Delegation
framework.description: Agents currently act indistinguishably from users — the industry hasn't even solved agent-as-distinct-identity yet, let alone multi-hop delegation. This confirms gap_6: production systems are using impersonation (shared credentials), not delegation chains.
framework.raw_quote: User impersonation by agents should be replaced by delegated authority. Currently, agents often act indistinguishably from users, creating accountability gaps and security risks.
